assertions.description: onPointerDown={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} present in the slider component
assertions.evidence: onPointerDown={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} present on <input type='range'> inside SliderComponent — noted with correct explanatory comment
if (context.payload instanceof SliderControl) { return SliderComponent as React.FC<...>; }
const render = new ReactPlugin<Schemes, AreaExtra>(); — no { createRoot } argument; this breaks with React 19 / strict-mode React 18
class SliderControl extends ClassicPreset.Control — extends the low-level Control base, not InputControl. Acceptable: ClassicPreset.Control provides the required id field.

notes: Dropped 1 point for missing {createRoot}. The baseline independently knew stopPropagation and instanceof discrimination but consistently forgets the React 19 createRoot requirement — a gap the skill specifically addresses.
